content: Next.js 16 renames middleware.ts to proxy.ts. The proxy file runs on the server before routes render, supports the Node.js runtime stably (was experimental pre-16), and is configured via a matcher (negative-match to exclude _next/static, images). Better Auth confirms: in proxy use auth.api.getSession({headers: await headers()}) for full DB-backed session validation, OR getSessionCookie(request) for a faster optimistic redirect that is NOT secure (must re-check auth in each route/Server Action). Auth checks in proxy should only redirect; resource-level enforcement belongs in Server Actions and pages.
